description: Slider and fine-tuning UX overhaul. Replace plain number inputs with scrub-enabled inputs, add slider+input combos, drag-on-label scrubbing, arrow key stepping, and better color pickers. Make precise adjustments intuitive like Figma/Photoshop.

userStories.title: Create ScrubInput component — drag-on-label to adjust values
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click-and-drag on a property label to scrub its numeric value, like Figma's inputs.
acceptanceCriteria: Create components/ui/scrub-input.tsx — a compound component with a label and numeric input
Clicking and dragging horizontally on the label changes the value proportionally
Default sensitivity: 1px drag = 1 unit change. Hold Shift for 10x speed. Hold Alt/Option for 0.1x precision
Cursor changes to ew-resize on label hover to indicate scrubbing is available
The label should highlight (blue text) while actively scrubbing
Value is committed on mouseup (pushes undo snapshot)
The input field next to the label still works as a normal editable number input
Arrow Up/Down in the input increments/decrements by step (default 1). Shift+Arrow = 10x step
Enter key commits the value. Escape reverts to the value before editing
Props: label, value, onChange, onCommit, min, max, step, precision (decimal places), suffix (like 'px' or '%' or '°')
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the foundation component. Use pointer capture for smooth drag across the screen. Track delta from pointerdown position. The label and input should be in a flex row. Styling: dark bg, compact h-7, text-xs. Match existing input styling.

userStories.title: Create SliderInput component — slider + number input combo
userStories.description: As a user, I want a slider with an editable number input for properties like opacity, corner radius, and rotation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create components/ui/slider-input.tsx — combines a Radix Slider with a ScrubInput
Slider and input are synced: dragging slider updates input, typing in input moves slider thumb
Slider fills available width, input has fixed width on the right
Supports min, max, step props that apply to both slider and input
onValueChange fires on every slider drag frame (for live preview)
onValueCommit fires on slider release and input blur/Enter (for undo snapshot)
Optional: show the label above with scrub-on-label functionality (reuses ScrubInput label logic)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Layout: label row on top (with scrub), slider below spanning full width, number input to the right of slider. Use the existing Slider component from components/ui/slider.tsx. The input should be w-14 to show 3-4 digits.

userStories.title: Replace transform inputs with ScrubInput — position, scale, rotation
userStories.description: As a user, I want to scrub position X/Y, scale, and rotation values by dragging on their labels.
acceptanceCriteria: X, Y inputs use ScrubInput with step=1, no min/max
Scale X, Scale Y use ScrubInput with step=0.01, min=0.01, precision=2
Rotation uses ScrubInput with step=1, suffix='°', wraps at 360
Scrubbing X/Y is fast: dragging the label moves the layer in real-time on the canvas
Undo snapshot is pushed once at the start of a scrub, not on every pixel
All existing keyboard input (typing values directly) still works
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Replace the current Input type=number fields in properties-panel.tsx (lines ~142-228). The onCommit callback should call pushSnapshot before the first change, then batch all scrub changes into one undo entry.

userStories.title: Replace opacity control with SliderInput
userStories.description: As a user, I want the opacity control to show a slider AND an editable input with scrub-on-label.
acceptanceCriteria: Opacity uses SliderInput with min=0, max=100, step=1, suffix='%'
Label shows 'Opacity' and is scrubbable
Slider shows the current value with a blue fill
Input shows the numeric percentage value and is directly editable
Real-time preview while scrubbing (no flicker)
Undo snapshot pushed on commit (slider release or input blur)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Replace the current Slider-only opacity control. Currently opacity shows 'Opacity — 75%' as label with just a slider below. The new layout should be the SliderInput combo.

userStories.title: Add SliderInput for shape corner radius
userStories.description: As a user, I want a slider for corner radius instead of just a number input.
acceptanceCriteria: Corner radius uses SliderInput with min=0, max=half the smaller dimension, step=1, suffix='px'
Max updates dynamically when shape size changes
The slider makes it easy to go from sharp corners (0) to fully rounded (max)
Existing number input behavior preserved
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Corner radius is currently a plain Input type=number. Adding a slider makes it much more discoverable and intuitive.

userStories.title: Add ScrubInput for text font size and spacing properties
userStories.description: As a user, I want to scrub font size, line height, and letter spacing by dragging on their labels.
acceptanceCriteria: Font Size uses ScrubInput with step=1, min=1, suffix='px'
Line Height uses ScrubInput with step=0.05, min=0.5, max=5, precision=2
Letter Spacing uses ScrubInput with step=0.1, min=-10, max=50, precision=1, suffix='px'
Scrubbing font size updates the canvas text in real-time
Scrubbing line height updates spacing between lines in real-time
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: These are the most commonly adjusted text properties. Scrub makes them much faster to fine-tune than typing numbers.

userStories.title: Add SliderInput for shape stroke width
userStories.description: As a user, I want a slider for stroke width to easily adjust it visually.
acceptanceCriteria: Stroke Width uses SliderInput with min=0, max=50, step=0.5, suffix='px'
Slider goes from 0 (no stroke visible) to 50px
Input allows typing larger values beyond slider max
Real-time canvas preview while scrubbing
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Stroke width is currently a plain number input. A slider helps users find the right width quickly.

userStories.title: Improve color picker — add HSL sliders and preset swatches
userStories.description: As a user, I want a better color picker with hue/saturation/lightness sliders and preset color swatches instead of just a hex input.
acceptanceCriteria: Create components/ui/color-picker.tsx as a popover panel
Shows a saturation/brightness square (SV plane) at the top
Hue slider below the square (full 0-360 spectrum bar)
Alpha/opacity slider below hue (checkerboard background showing transparency)
Hex input at the bottom for direct entry (with # prefix)
Row of 8-10 preset swatches: black, white, red, orange, yellow, green, blue, purple, pink, gray
Clicking a swatch applies that color immediately
The current native <input type=color> is replaced with this custom picker
Popover opens on click of color swatch, closes on outside click
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The current color picker is a native browser <input type=color> which is ugly and inconsistent across browsers. A custom HSL picker gives us full control. Render the SV square using a canvas or CSS gradient overlay. Hue bar is a horizontal rainbow gradient slider.

userStories.title: Add input focus management — select-all on focus, tab between inputs
userStories.description: As a user, I want clicking a number input to select all text so I can immediately type a new value, and Tab to move between inputs.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking/focusing a ScrubInput or SliderInput number field selects all text
Tab key moves focus to the next input in the properties panel
Shift+Tab moves to the previous input
Inputs don't steal keyboard shortcuts while focused (already handled by US-310)
Clicking outside any input returns focus to the canvas
Double-click on a number input does NOT start scrubbing — it selects text for editing
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Select-all-on-focus is standard in design tools. It means you can click an input and immediately type '42' to replace the value, instead of having to select-all manually first. Add tabIndex ordering to inputs.

userStories.title: Add dimension inputs with constrain proportions toggle for shapes
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see and edit width/height of shapes with a lock icon to constrain proportions.
acceptanceCriteria: Shape layers show Width and Height ScrubInputs with step=1, min=1, suffix='px'
A chain/link icon between W and H toggles proportional constraint
When constrained: changing width adjusts height proportionally, and vice versa
Constraint state is stored per-session (not per-layer) as a UI preference
Scrubbing width/height updates the shape on canvas in real-time
The inputs show the actual pixel dimensions (width * scaleX, height * scaleY)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Currently shape dimensions are shown as read-only. Making them editable with proportional constraints is a big UX win. The chain icon should be a small toggle between the W and H rows — on when constrained (blue), off when independent (gray).

userStories.title: Improve adjust panel sliders with value input and reset-on-double-click
userStories.description: As a user, I want the adjust panel sliders to have editable value inputs and reset to default on double-click.
acceptanceCriteria: Each adjust slider (brightness, contrast, etc.) uses SliderInput with its current min/max/step
Double-clicking the slider track or label resets to the default value
The X reset button is still available for non-default values
Value input shows the current numeric value and is directly editable
Labels are scrubbable (drag to adjust)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The adjust panel already uses Slider + value display. Upgrade to SliderInput for consistency and add double-click-to-reset UX pattern.

userStories.title: Add rotation dial — visual rotation control
userStories.description: As a user, I want a small circular dial next to the rotation input for intuitive angle adjustment.
acceptanceCriteria: Create components/ui/rotation-dial.tsx — a 32x32px circular SVG control
A small dot on the circle indicates the current angle
Click-and-drag around the circle to rotate (maps angle from center of dial)
Hold Shift while dragging to snap to 15° increments
The dial is shown next to the rotation ScrubInput in the properties panel
Dial and input stay synced
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: A visual rotation dial gives immediate spatial feedback about the current angle. The dial is small and complementary to the numeric input. Use Math.atan2(dy, dx) from the dial center to compute the angle from pointer position.
